translations.literal: The Huainanzi says, 'Not follow a single rut's road, nor keep one corner's finger-pointing, without shifting with the vulgar.'"
translations.idiomatic: Huainanzi: do not rut in one track nor point one dogma—move with the age.'"

sentences.zh: 校勘記

translations.literal: Collation notes
translations.idiomatic: Section title: Collation notes.

sentences.zh: 一六二九頁七行充幼聰朗按：汲本、殿本「朗」作「明」。

translations.literal: Page 1629 line 7: 'In youth Chong was bright and quick-witted.' Note: Jiben and Dianben use a different graph for the second character of the binome meaning cleverness.
translations.idiomatic: Collation: the Palace woodblocks write the cleverness binome with the graph meaning 'enlighten' where other editions use the graph meaning 'clear.'

sentences.zh: 一六三一頁四行夫五*

translations.literal: Page 1631 line 4: the phrase begins 'As for the five—' with a manuscript asterisk.
translations.idiomatic: Collation line marking the broken phrase about the five ages or dynasties.

translations.literal: (generations)
translations.idiomatic: The parenthesis supplies the alternate gloss 'generations' for the preceding lacuna.

sentences.zh: **[代]*之臣刊誤謂此「世」字當是「代」字，後人誤改。

translations.literal: **[dynasties]* ministers: Kanwu states the graph for 'age' here should read 'dynasty,' wrongly altered by later hands.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu argues the word for reign-generation was corrupted into the word for world-age; later copyists erred.

sentences.zh: 今據以回改。

translations.literal: Now emended accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Text restored per note.

sentences.zh: 一六三一頁七行是故德不稱其禍必酷能不稱其殃必大刊誤謂「德不稱」下脫「其位」二字，「能不稱」下脫「其祿」二字。

translations.literal: Page 1631 line 7: Kanwu: after 'virtue does not match' the two graphs for 'its position' are missing; after 'ability does not match' the two graphs for 'its stipend' are missing.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu supplies the missing objects—office for virtue, stipend for ability—that the sentence requires.

sentences.zh: 按：集解引蘇輿說，謂潛夫論貴忠篇作「德不稱其任」，「能不稱其位」。

translations.literal: Note: Jijie cites Su Yu that the Qianfu lun chapter 'Exalting Loyalty' reads 'virtue does not match its charge' and 'ability does not match its station.'
translations.idiomatic: Su Yu's parallel in the Qianfu lun uses charge and station as the complements instead of bare verbs.

sentences.zh: 一六三二頁九行歷觀前政貴人之用心也按：集解引蘇輿說，謂潛夫論「政」作「世」，連下讀，疑此避唐諱改。

translations.literal: Page 1632 line 9: 'Surveying the intentions of the great men of earlier reigns.' Note: Jijie cites Su Yu that Qianfu lun writes 'age' for 'reign' and reads on—suspect Tang taboo change here.
translations.idiomatic: Su Yu thinks the original read 'former ages' in one word order and that the received 'reign' reflects Tang avoidance of the imperial name.

sentences.zh: 一六三四頁一三行懷憂憤憤按：殿本「憤憤」作「憤憒」，今潛夫論亦作「憤憒」。

translations.literal: Page 1634 line 13: 'bearing anxiety and pent-up anger.' Note: Dianben writes a different second graph in the reduplication; modern Qianfu lun matches Dianben.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace edition's reduplication differs from the Qianfu lun wording; editors align with that text.

sentences.zh: 一六三四頁一五行此妖妄之甚者也按：「妖」原作「□」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1634 line 15: 'this is the height of delusion and falsehood.' Note: the graph for uncanny was originally a lacuna; emended directly.
translations.idiomatic: The manuscript gap was filled with the graph meaning uncanny or demonic.

sentences.zh: 一六三五頁四行用功千倍按：集解引蘇輿說，謂「千倍」當從元書作「十倍」。

translations.literal: Page 1635 line 4: 'a thousandfold the labor.' Note: Jijie cites Su Yu that 'thousandfold' should follow the Yuan text as 'tenfold.'
translations.idiomatic: Su Yu argues the multiplier was ten, not a thousand, in the older recension.

sentences.zh: 一六三五頁一０行車軿數里汲本「軿」作「駢」。

translations.literal: Page 1635 line 10: 'carriages ping for miles.' Note: Jiben writes the homophone meaning paired horses for the graph meaning curtained carriage.
translations.idiomatic: One woodblock tradition prefers the paired-horses graph over the curtained-carriage graph.

sentences.zh: 校補謂車駢數里本指車馬言。

translations.literal: Collation supplement: 'carriages and horses for miles' originally referred to carriages and horses.
translations.idiomatic: Supplement: phrase describes carriages.

sentences.zh: 作「軿」者誤，章懷注亦誤。

translations.literal: The collation supplement calls the curtained-carriage reading wrong and says Zhang Hua's commentary is also wrong.
translations.idiomatic: That supplement rejects the curtained-carriage reading and faults Zhang Hua's gloss.

sentences.zh: 今按：下言「緹帷竟道」，明指車言，作「軿」者是，校補說非。

translations.literal: Editor's note: Below it says 'crimson curtains shade the whole road,' clearly referring to carriages—writing ping for the curtained carriage is correct; the supplement is wrong.
translations.idiomatic: The modern editor answers the supplement: the following line about crimson curtains proves the curtained-carriage reading.

sentences.zh: 一六三七頁四行春秋以為*

translations.literal: Page 1637 line 4: Chunqiu thought it *
translations.idiomatic: Collation note on the Spring and Autumn judgment line ending with an asterisk.

translations.literal: (wrong)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss marks the negative particle supplied in the lacuna.

sentences.zh: **[不]*君殿本「非」作「不」，與左傳合，今據改。

translations.literal: **[unworthy]* of a ruler: Dianben writes the shorter negative particle, matching the Zuo Commentary; emended per this.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace edition's negative matches the Zuo wording; editors adopt it.

sentences.zh: 一六三七頁四行樂舉按：潛夫論作「樂呂」，成二年左傳作「樂舉」，文十八年、宣二年並作「樂呂」。

translations.literal: Page 1637 line 4: 'Yue Ju.' Note: Qianfu lun writes a different second syllable; Cheng second year Zuo has Ju; Wen eighteenth and Xuan second years both use the alternate syllable.
translations.idiomatic: The minister's name appears with two variant second syllables across the Qianfu lun and Zuo parallels.

sentences.zh: 一六三七頁七行葬南巴之中按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂墨子節葬篇「南巴之中」作「南己之巿」。

translations.literal: Page 1637 line 7: 'buried amid Southern Ba.' Note: Jijie cites Shen Qinhan that Mozi 'Moderate Burial' writes 'Southern Ji's market' for 'Southern Ba.'
translations.idiomatic: Mozi names a different southern market; the place-name graphs were confused.

sentences.zh: 呂覽安死篇「舜葬於紀巿，不變其肆」。

translations.literal: Lüshi chunqiu 'Peaceful Death' chapter: 'Shun was buried at Ji's market without disturbing the stalls.'
translations.idiomatic: The Lüshi parallel places the grave at Ji market with stalls still open.

sentences.zh: 高注「九疑山亦有紀邑」。

translations.literal: Gao's commentary: 'Ji town also exists on Mount Jiuyi.'
translations.idiomatic: Gao You locates the Ji placename on Mount Jiuyi.

sentences.zh: 己與巴相似而誤。

translations.literal: Ji was mistaken for Ba because the graphs resemble.
translations.idiomatic: Copyists confused two similar place-name graphs.

sentences.zh: 一六三九頁六行出處默語按：殿本「默語」作「語默」。

translations.literal: Page 1639 line 6: 'whether retiring or outspoken.' Note: Dianben reverses the word order to speech first, silence second.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace edition transposes the silence-and-speech binome.

sentences.zh: 一六三九頁一三行化國之日舒以長按：潛夫論「化」作「治」，此亦避唐諱改。

translations.literal: Page 1639 line 13: 'In a well-governed state the days are long.' Note: Qianfu lun writes 'govern' for 'transform'—here also suspect Tang taboo change.
translations.idiomatic: The Qianfu lun uses the verb to govern where this text reads transform—likely Tang taboo substitution.

sentences.zh: 惠棟謂唐諱「治」，章懷注後漢書，隨文改易，此篇「治國之日舒以長」，改為「化國」，後人因之，遂有「光天化日」之語，豈非郢書而燕說乎？

translations.literal: Hui Dong: Tang taboo on the verb to govern; Zhang Hua's Hou Han notes altered this piece's 'In a governed state the days are long' to 'transformed state,' later folk coined a four-character idiom about bright daylight—does that not become Ying writing and Yan explaining?
translations.idiomatic: Hui Dong traces how taboo-driven substitution spawned a folk proverb detached from the original sense.

sentences.zh: 一六四０頁四行*

translations.literal: Page 1640 line 4: *
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna marker *

translations.literal: (order)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss marks the graph for command or order supplied in the lacuna.

sentences.zh: **[今]*冤民仰希申訴刊誤謂案文「令」當作「今」。

translations.literal: **[Now]* wronged people look up hoping to lodge complaints: Kanwu states by the text the graph for command should read now.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu corrects a copyist's confusion between now and command.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Emended accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Applied emendation.

sentences.zh: 一六四０頁一六行不橈故無恩於吏「橈」原作「撓」，逕據殿本改。

translations.literal: Page 1640 line 16: 'Not cringing therefore wins no favor from the clerk'—the graph for unbending was originally written with the hand radical; emended per Dianben.
translations.idiomatic: Editors normalize to the wood-radical graph for unbending, per the Palace edition.

sentences.zh: 按：撓橈從手從木，古互通，然上文既作「橈」，以改歸一律為是。

translations.literal: Note: the two graphs for bend with hand or wood radical interchange in antiquity, yet since above already uses the wood form, uniform emendation is appropriate.
translations.idiomatic: The note explains why the hand-radical form was swapped for the wood-radical form for consistency.

sentences.zh: 一六四二頁一二行頤育萬民按：汲本、殿本「民」作「物」。

translations.literal: Page 1642 line 12: 'to nurture the myriad people.' Note: Jiben and Dianben write 'things' for 'people.'
translations.idiomatic: Some Palace editions read the myriad things instead of the myriad people.

sentences.zh: 一六四四頁五行欲*[南]*奔*

translations.literal: Page 1644 line 5: wish *[south]* to flee *
translations.idiomatic: Collation note on the line about fleeing south, with asterisks marking lacunae.

translations.literal: (south)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss marks the cardinal south supplied in the lacuna.

sentences.zh: *荊州張森楷校勘記謂州名有「南」字，始見宋志，漢、魏、晉俱無，此「南」字當在「奔」字上。

translations.literal: *to Jing Province: Zhang Senkai's collation states the word south belongs before flee; Han Wei Jin texts never prefix the province with south.
translations.idiomatic: Zhang Senkai argues the south modifier should precede the verb flee, not the province name.

sentences.zh: 按：魏志袁紹傳正作「欲南奔荊州」，今據改。

translations.literal: Note: Wei zhi Yuan Shao zhuan correctly reads 'wished to flee south to Jing Province'; emended per this.
translations.idiomatic: The Wei chronicle's word order is adopted as authoritative.

sentences.zh: 一六四五頁一三行騰蛇有鱗按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂爾雅釋魚「騰」作「螣」，無「有鱗」二字。

translations.literal: Page 1645 line 13: 'Soaring serpent has scales.' Note: Jijie cites Shen Qinhan that Erya 'Fish' writes a different first graph and omits the phrase about scales.
translations.idiomatic: The Erya fish chapter uses another graph for the serpent and lacks the scales clause.

sentences.zh: 一六四五頁一三行有角曰龍按：集解引沉欽韓說，謂廣雅「有角曰虯龍」，注脫「虯」字。

translations.literal: Page 1645 line 13: 'What has horns is called a dragon.' Note: Jijie cites Shen Qinhan that Guangya reads horned dragon with an extra graph—commentary omits that graph.
translations.idiomatic: The Guangya lemma names a horned dragon; a graph dropped out of this commentary.

sentences.zh: 一六四六頁一行抗志山棲按：汲本、殿本「棲」作「西」。

translations.literal: Page 1646 line 1: 'Set your will on mountain seclusion.' Note: Jiben and Dianben write west for seclusion.
translations.idiomatic: Some editions miswrite mountain seclusion as mountain west.

sentences.zh: 一六四六頁二行微風為柂按：「柂」原斗「杝」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1646 line 2: 'the light breeze your rudder'—the rudder graph was originally miswritten as a similar tree graph; emended directly.
translations.idiomatic: Editors fix a tree-radical misprint to the rudder graph.

sentences.zh: 注同。

translations.literal: Commentary likewise.
translations.idiomatic: Same fix in commentary.

sentences.zh: 一六四七頁一五行政亂從此周復按：王先謙謂「政」亦「治」字避諱改。

translations.literal: Page 1647 line 15: 'order and chaos revolve in cycles.' Note: Wang Xianqian states 'government' here is also taboo for 'govern.'
translations.idiomatic: Wang Xianqian argues that the graph zheng in this line likewise substitutes for zhi because Tang editors tabooed the emperor's personal name, so the original wording likely read zhi luan.

sentences.zh: 一六四八頁三行蓋雜伎樂*

translations.literal: Page 1648 line 3: 'miscellaneous entertainments *
translations.idiomatic: Collation line about miscellaneous entertainments, ending with an asterisk.

translations.literal: (with)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss supplies the preposition meaning with or by means of.

sentences.zh: **[也]*據漢書武帝紀文穎注改。

translations.literal: **[also]*' emended per Wenying's commentary to Han shu Emperor Wu's annals.
translations.idiomatic: The reading follows Wenying's gloss in the Han shu commentary tradition.

sentences.zh: 一六四八頁四行宋音宴安溺志按：禮記樂記「安」作「女」。

translations.literal: Page 1648 line 4: Song music ease drowns the will.' Note: Yue ji writes woman for ease.
translations.idiomatic: The Record of Music uses the graph for daughter or woman where this text reads ease.

sentences.zh: 一六四八頁一一行倡謳*

translations.literal: Page 1648 line 11: singing girls*
translations.idiomatic: Collation note on the line ending with singers and jesters, marked with an asterisk in the manuscript.

translations.literal: (courtesans)
translations.idiomatic: The interlinear gloss identifies the missing graph as the character for female entertainers or courtesans.

sentences.zh: **[伎]*樂據集解本改。

translations.literal: ** entertainers' emended per Jijie edition.
translations.idiomatic: The entertainers graph is restored from the Jijie recension.

sentences.zh: 一六五０頁五行道三十四按：集解引洪亮吉說，謂前書地理志「三十四」作「三十二」。

translations.literal: Page 1650 line 5: 'thirty-four routes.' Note: Jijie cites Hong Liangji that Former Han treatise on geography writes thirty-two.
translations.idiomatic: Hong Liangji counts thirty-two routes in the Former Han geography, not thirty-four.

sentences.zh: 一六五０頁五行南北一萬三百六十八里按：集解引王鳴盛說，謂「南北一萬」下前書有「三千」字，此脫。

translations.literal: Page 1650 line 5: 'north-south ten thousand three hundred sixty-eight li.' Note: Jijie cites Wang Mingsheng that Former Han before 'ten thousand' has 'three thousand'—omitted here.
translations.idiomatic: Wang Mingsheng, cited in the collected commentary, notes that the Former Han geography entry inserts the two graphs for three thousand immediately after north-south ten thousand, and those graphs have dropped out in this copy.

sentences.zh: 一六五０頁一五行假之以殺生之權按：汲本、殿本作「生殺之權」。

translations.literal: Page 1650 line 15: 'lending them the power of life and death.' Note: Jiben and Dianben read 'power over life and killing.'
translations.idiomatic: Palace editions transpose life and killing in the binome.

sentences.zh: 一六五二頁一０行是為忍於殺人*

translations.literal: Page 1652 line 10: 'that would mean sparing the murderer *
translations.idiomatic: Collation line on sparing murderers, ending with an asterisk.

translations.literal: (indeed)
translations.idiomatic: The particle ye in parentheses marks a variant or editorial seal on the preceding clause.

sentences.zh: *而不忍於刑人也據刊誤刪。

translations.literal: *yet being ruthless toward corporal punishment'—deleted per Kanwu.
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu deletes the dangling clause as spurious.

sentences.zh: 一六五六頁七行*

translations.literal: Page 1656 line 7: *
translations.idiomatic: Line marker *

translations.literal: (salary)
translations.idiomatic: The gloss in parentheses supplies the graph lu meaning stipend or salary.

sentences.zh: *班*[祿]*未定刊誤謂案文當作「班祿」。

translations.literal: *rank and * salary * unsettled: Kanwu states the text should read 'rank and salary.'
translations.idiomatic: Kanwu restores the binome meaning fixed stipend ranks.

sentences.zh: 今據改。

translations.literal: Emended accordingly.
translations.idiomatic: Applied.

sentences.zh: 一六五六頁一二行子之道貊*[道]*也據汲本補，與今本孟子合。

translations.literal: Page 1656 line 12: 'Your way is the way of the Mo'—second 'way' supplied per Jiben, matching modern Mencius.
translations.idiomatic: The Jiben woodblock restores the second dao character in the line so that the sentence matches the wording of the Mencius as it circulates today.

sentences.zh: 一六五六頁一二行趙岐注雲按：原本趙岐之「岐」皆作「歧」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1656 line 12: 'Zhao Qi's commentary says'—original editions all wrote the commentator's name with the branch graph for forked path; emended directly.
translations.idiomatic: Editors normalize the commentator's name to the standard spelling with the mount graph.

sentences.zh: 一六五七頁一四行而猶展申徒嘉之志按：汲本、殿本「徒」作「屠」。

translations.literal: Page 1657 line 14: 'yet still extended Shen Tu Jia's resolve.' Note: Jiben and Dianben write the second graph of the double surname as butcher rather than wayfarer.
translations.idiomatic: Palace editions vary the second graph of the chancellor's compound surname.

sentences.zh: 一六五九頁二行*

translations.literal: Page 1659 line 2: *
(them)
translations.idiomatic: The parenthetical graph records the pronoun zhi as the reading adopted in the revised text.

sentences.zh: **[上]*不使人捽抑而刑之也據殿本改，與前書賈誼傳合。

translations.literal: **[above]* not send men to seize and mutilate them' emended per Dianben, matching Former Han Jia Yi zhuan.
translations.idiomatic: The Palace reading aligns with the Former Han biography of Jia Yi.

sentences.zh: 一六六二頁四行言其詳約不同按：「詳」原斗「群」，逕改正。

translations.literal: Page 1662 line 4: 'showing how detail and brevity differ.' Note: 'detail' was originally miswritten as flock; emended directly.
translations.idiomatic: A flock graph was misread as the graph for detail.

sentences.zh: 又按：汲本、殿本作「言其詳約也」，無「不同」二字。

translations.literal: Further note: Jiben and Dianben read 'speaking of its detail and brevity' without the two graphs 'not the same.'
translations.idiomatic: Some editions omit the final two graphs meaning not the same.
